perturbation.original_text: The term “Confidential Information” shall include all Hydranautics’ trade secrets and information that conc erns the confidential business or affairs of Hydranautics and Nitto including without limitation, financial information and reports, business plans, design and construction plans, manufacturing processes, customer lists, employee data, forecasts, strategies, and all other proprietary business information.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Confidential Information” shall include only marked documents and verbally communicated trade secrets identified by Hydranautics representatives during the tour.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information'. Originally, it broadly encompassed all Hydranautics' trade secrets and business information. The modified definition limits it to only what is explicitly marked or verbally identified, creating a contradiction if Section 2 refers to the broader, original definition of 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Visitor agrees and acknowledges that in the course of, or incident to, the tour of Hydranautics’ facilities, Hydranutics may provide to, or Visitor will otherwise become exposed to Hydranautics’ Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only marked documents and verbally communicated trade secrets. The contradicted text states that the visitor will be exposed to Hydranautics' Confidential Information during the tour. Because the original definition of Confidential Information was much broader, the changed text creates a contradiction. Under the new definition, the visitor might be exposed to information that would have been considered confidential under the original definition, but is no longer considered confidential because it isn't marked or verbally identified. This means the Visitor is no longer bound by the same confidentiality obligations as before, hence a direct contradiction and impacting the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Visitor agrees to hold the Confidential Information in the strictest confidence, and will exercise at least the same care with respect thereto as it exercises with respect to its’ own confidential or proprietary information and will not without Hydranautics’ consent use, divulge, co py, release, sell, loan or otherwise reveal such Confidential Information to others.
perturbation.changed_text: Visitor agrees to make reasonable efforts to protect information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' during the tour.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause imposes a stringent duty to protect 'Confidential Information'. The modified version weakens this obligation to 'reasonable efforts' for explicitly marked information, potentially contradicting the broader confidentiality commitment implied in other sections, particularly if Section 1 is now interpreted narrowly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Visitor understands that his or her breach of this Agreement may subject him or her and his or her employees to liability.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the strictest confidence for all Confidential Information. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'reasonable efforts' for explicitly marked information. This creates a contradiction because Section 1, as altered, implies that information not explicitly marked as 'Confidential' during the tour is subject to a lower standard of protection, contradicting the broad liability provision in the Contradicted Text which assumes any breach of the agreement, including those related to confidential information, may result in liability. Therefore, a breach of unmarked confidential information may or may not subject the visitor to liability depending on how the modified Section 1 is interpreted, causing a direct conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Visitor will not photograph, vid eotape, or otherwise make any record of or preserve any Confidential Information to which he/she may be given access during the facility tour(s).
perturbation.changed_text: Visitor will make reasonable efforts not to photograph or videotape areas specifically designated as off-limits during the facility tour(s).
perturbation.explanation: The original clause strictly prohibits any recording of Confidential Information. The modification introduces ambiguity by limiting the prohibition to 'areas specifically designated as off-limits,' implying that recording outside these areas is permissible, which contradicts the intent of protecting Confidential Information, especially if the definition in section 1 is later used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “Confidential Information” shall include all Hydranautics’ trade secrets and information that conc erns the confidential business or affairs of Hydranautics and Nitto including without limitation, financial information and reports, business plans, design and construction plans, manufacturing processes, customer lists, employee data, forecasts, strategies, and all other proprietary business information.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits any recording of Confidential Information. The changed text allows recording in areas not 'specifically designated as off-limits'. This creates a contradiction because the 'Confidential Information' defined in the contradicted text could be present in areas not designated as off-limits, thus contradicting the original intent to protect all Confidential Information as defined. This fits the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Visitor will abide by all safety regulations, stay within designated areas of the facility per the established tour, and will not leave the touring party without an escort by a Hydranautics authorized employee.
perturbation.changed_text: Visitor agrees to generally adhere to safety guidelines and stay with the tour group to the best of their ability.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates strict adherence to safety regulations and tour guidelines. The modified version weakens this to a 'best of their ability' standard, introducing uncertainty and potentially conflicting with other sections that assume strict compliance, making it harder to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Visitor understands that his or her breach of this Agreement may subject him or her and his or her employees to liability.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates strict adherence to safety regulations, while the changed text weakens this to a 'best of their ability' standard. This introduces uncertainty. The contradicted text states that a breach of the agreement may subject the visitor to liability. The relaxed standard in the changed text makes it harder to enforce the 'breach of agreement' clause because what constitutes a breach is now ambiguous. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agre ement between the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the primary understanding between the parties regarding the facility tour, but may be supplemented by verbal instructions.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause declares the agreement as the entire agreement. The modified version introduces the possibility of 'supplemental verbal instructions', which contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause and creates uncertainty about what constitutes the complete agreement, especially in relation to confidential information, contradicting section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Visitor agrees to hold the Confidential Information in the strictest confidence, and will exercise at least the same care with respect thereto as it exercises with respect to its’ own confidential or proprietary information and will not without Hydranautics’ consent use, divulge, co py, release, sell, loan or otherwise reveal such Confidential Information to others.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement is the 'entire Agreement'. The changed text introduces the possibility of 'supplemental verbal instructions', which contradicts the original clause and creates uncertainty about what constitutes the complete agreement, especially in relation to confidential information, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. This directly impacts section 3, which details the handling of confidential information, because it is now unclear whether these 'supplemental verbal instructions' are also subject to the confidentiality agreement. Therefore, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Visitor understands that his or her breach of this Agreement may subject him or her and his or her employees to liability.
perturbation.changed_text: Visitor acknowledges that a deliberate and malicious breach of this agreement may result in legal action.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that any breach may subject the visitor to liability. The modified version narrows this to a 'deliberate and malicious breach', increasing the burden of proof and potentially contradicting the original intention of strict adherence to the agreement terms. If another section states that even inadvertent disclosure is a breach, this creates an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Visitor agrees to hold the Confidential Information in the strictest confidence, and will exercise at least the same care with respect thereto as it exercises with respect to its’ own confidential or proprietary information and will not without Hydranautics’ consent use, divulge, co py, release, sell, loan or otherwise reveal such Confidential Information to others.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that any breach may subject the visitor to liability. The changed text narrows this to a 'deliberate and malicious breach', increasing the burden of proof. The contradicted text requires the visitor to hold Confidential Information in the strictest confidence and exercise the same care as they would with their own confidential information. If the visitor inadvertently divulges confidential information, according to the contradicted text, this constitutes a breach. However, under the changed text, this inadvertent disclosure would not trigger liability unless it was also deliberate and malicious, creating a contradiction. Therefore, based on the Perturbation Explanation and the contradiction definition for Omissions, there is a strong in-text contradiction.
